code: "/* Orbit camera for chess board — matches source OrbitControls behavior */\r

  \r

  // Spherical coordinates\r

  let game;\r

  let azimuth = 0;\r

  let polar = 0;\r

  let distance = 0;\r

  \r

  // Target values for smooth interpolation\r

  let targetAzimuth = 0;\r

  let targetPolar = 0;\r

  let targetDistance = 0;\r

  \r

  // Drag state\r

  let isDragging = false;\r

  let lastPointerX = 0;\r

  let lastPointerY = 0;\r

  \r

  // Pre-allocated vectors\r

  let camPos = new THREE.Vector3();\r

  let lookTarget = new THREE.Vector3(0, 0, 0);\r

  \r

  // Event handlers\r

  let onPointerDownFn = null;\r

  let onPointerMoveFn = null;\r

  let onPointerUpFn = null;\r

  let onWheelFn = null;\r

  let canvas = null;\r

  \r

  function updateCameraPosition(camera, smooth) {\r

  \  let lerp = smooth;\r

  \  azimuth += (targetAzimuth - azimuth) * lerp;\r

  \  polar += (targetPolar - polar) * lerp;\r

  \  distance += (targetDistance - distance) * lerp;\r

  \r

  \  let sinP = Math.sin(polar);\r

  \  let cosP = Math.cos(polar);\r

  \  let sinA = Math.sin(azimuth);\r

  \  let cosA = Math.cos(azimuth);\r

  \r

  \  camPos.set(\r

  \    distance * sinP * cosA + lookTarget.x,\r

  \    distance * cosP + lookTarget.y,\r

  \    distance * sinP * sinA + lookTarget.z\r

  \  );\r

  \r

  \  camera.position.copy(camPos);\r

  \  camera.lookAt(lookTarget);\r

  }\r

  \r

  this.init = function (_game) {\r

  \  game = _game;\r

  \  azimuth = this.getAttribute(\"initialAzimuthAngle\");\r

  \  polar = this.getAttribute(\"initialPolarAngle\");\r

  \  distance = this.getAttribute(\"initialDistance\");\r

  \  targetAzimuth = azimuth;\r

  \  targetPolar = polar;\r

  \  targetDistance = distance;\r

  \  lookTarget.y = this.getAttribute(\"lookAtY\");\r

  \r

  \  // Set FOV\r

  \  if (game.camera && game.camera.fov !== undefined) {\r

  \    game.camera.fov = this.getAttribute(\"fov\");\r

  \    game.camera.updateProjectionMatrix();\r

  \  }\r

  \r

  \  // Set initial position immediately (no smoothing)\r

  \  if (game.camera) {\r

  \    updateCameraPosition(game.camera, 1.0);\r

  \  }\r

  \r

  \  // Input setup — use the engine renderer's canvas only (no DOM lookup).\r

  \  canvas = (game.renderer && game.renderer.domElement) || null;\r

  \  if (!canvas) return;\r

  \r

  \  const pointerDown = [];\r

  \  onPointerDownFn = (e) => {\r

  \    // Only orbit with left mouse button or single touch\r

  \    if (e.button !== undefined && e.button !== 0) return;\r

  \    isDragging = true;\r

  \    lastPointerX = e.clientX;\r

  \    lastPointerY = e.clientY;\r

  \    pointerDown.push(e);\r

  \  };\r

  \r

  \  let prevDiff = 0;\r

  \  onPointerMoveFn = (e) => {\r

  \    if (!isDragging) return;\r

  \r

  \    if (pointerDown.length === 1) {\r

  \      let dx = e.clientX - lastPointerX;\r

  \      let dy = e.clientY - lastPointerY;\r

  \      lastPointerX = e.clientX;\r

  \      lastPointerY = e.clientY;\r

  \r

  \      targetAzimuth -= dx * this.getAttribute(\"rotateSpeed\") * -1; // reverse x-axis\r

  \      targetPolar -= dy * this.getAttribute(\"rotateSpeed\");\r

  \r

  \      // Clamp polar angle\r

  \      targetPolar = Math.max(this.getAttribute(\"minPolarAngle\"), Math.min(this.getAttribute(\"maxPolarAngle\"), targetPolar));\r

  \r

  \    } else if (pointerDown.length === 2) { // Pinch\r

  \      const index = pointerDown.findIndex(\r

  \        (cachedEv) => cachedEv.pointerId === e.pointerId,\r

  \      );\r

  \      pointerDown[index] = e;\r

  \r

  \      const curDiff = Math.hypot(\r

  \        pointerDown[0].clientX - pointerDown[1].clientX,\r

  \        pointerDown[0].clientY - pointerDown[1].clientY,\r

  \      );\r

  \r

  \      if (prevDiff > 0) {\r

  \        if (curDiff > prevDiff) {\r

  \          targetDistance += -curDiff * this.getAttribute(\"zoomSpeed\") * 0.01;\r

  \          targetDistance = Math.max(this.getAttribute(\"minDistance\"), Math.min(this.getAttribute(\"maxDistance\"), targetDistance));\r

  \        }\r

  \        if (curDiff < prevDiff) {\r

  \          targetDistance += curDiff * this.getAttribute(\"zoomSpeed\") * 0.01;\r

  \          targetDistance = Math.max(this.getAttribute(\"minDistance\"), Math.min(this.getAttribute(\"maxDistance\"), targetDistance));\r

  \        }\r

  \      }\r

  \r

  \      // Cache the distance for the next move event\r

  \      prevDiff = curDiff;\r

  \    }\r

  \  };\r

  \r

  \  onPointerUpFn = (e) => {\r

  \    isDragging = false;\r

  \r

  \    if (pointerDown.length === 0) return;\r

  \    const index = pointerDown.findIndex(\r

  \      (cachedEv) => cachedEv.pointerId === e.pointerId,\r

  \    );\r

  \    pointerDown.splice(index, 1);\r

  \r

  \    if (pointerDown.length < 2) {\r

  \      prevDiff = -1;\r

  \    }\r

  \  };\r

  \r

  \  onWheelFn = (e) => {\r

  \    e.preventDefault();\r

  \    targetDistance += e.deltaY * this.getAttribute(\"zoomSpeed\") * 0.01;\r

  \    targetDistance = Math.max(this.getAttribute(\"minDistance\"), Math.min(this.getAttribute(\"maxDistance\"), targetDistance));\r

  \  };\r

  \r

  \  canvas.addEventListener(\"pointerdown\", onPointerDownFn) // @check-ok\r

  \  canvas.addEventListener(\"pointermove\", onPointerMoveFn); // @check-ok\r

  \  canvas.addEventListener(\"pointerup\", onPointerUpFn) // @check-ok\r

  \  canvas.addEventListener(\"pointerleave\", onPointerUpFn); // @check-ok\r

  \  canvas.addEventListener(\"wheel\", onWheelFn, { passive: false }); // @check-ok\r

  };\r

  \r

  this.update = function (deltaTime) {\r

  \  if (!game || !game.camera) return;\r

  \  updateCameraPosition(game.camera, this.getAttribute(\"smoothing\"));\r

  };\r

  \r

  this.dispose = function () {\r

  \  if (canvas) {\r

  \    if (onPointerDownFn) canvas.removeEventListener(\"pointerdown\", onPointerDownFn) // @check-ok complex input\r

  \    if (onPointerMoveFn) canvas.removeEventListener(\"pointermove\", onPointerMoveFn);\r

  \    if (onPointerUpFn) {\r

  \      canvas.removeEventListener(\"pointerup\", onPointerUpFn) // @check-ok complex input\r

  \      canvas.removeEventListener(\"pointerleave\", onPointerUpFn);\r

  \    }\r

  \    if (onWheelFn) canvas.removeEventListener(\"wheel\", onWheelFn);\r

  \  }\r

  \  onPointerDownFn = null;\r

  \  onPointerMoveFn = null;\r

  \  onPointerUpFn = null;\r

  \  onWheelFn = null;\r

  \  canvas = null;\r

  };\r

  \r

  this.onEvent = function (msg, data) {\r

  \  if (msg === \"resetCamera\") {\r

  \    azimuth = -Math.PI;\r

  \    polar = Math.PI / 3;\r

  \    distance = 14;\r

  \    targetAzimuth = azimuth;\r

  \    targetPolar = polar;\r

  \    targetDistance = distance;\r

  \  }\r

  };"

# Machine Arena Low-Level Design

## Object Hierarchy

```text
Default Scene
├── DefaultCamera (cameraType=NONE, driven by ma.machineArena)
├── Player (tag=Player, invisible capsule, mirrored to FPS camera position)
├── GameRoot
│   └── ma.machineArena
│       ├── MA_RuntimeWorld (runtimeOnly)
│       ├── MA_Enemies (runtimeOnly)
│       ├── MA_Projectiles (runtimeOnly)
│       ├── MA_VFX (runtimeOnly)
│       └── MA_WeaponView (runtimeOnly, parented to camera)
└── UI Camera
    └── MA_UIRoot (UIKit Fullscreen)
```

## Component Architecture

| Behavior/Lambda | Attached To | Responsibility | Pattern |
|-----------------|-------------|----------------|---------|
| `ma.machineArena` | `GameRoot` | Owns FPS camera, input, procedural arena, weapons, enemies, waves, upgrades, fragments, shop, HUD, audio, and cleanup | Consolidated source-loop port |

## Data Flow Per Game Event

### Event: Run Start

```text
Menu input -> startRun() -> resetRun() -> buildArena() -> startNextWave() -> phase="playing"
```

### Event: Player Movement

```text
InputManager motion/actions -> updatePlayer(dt) -> mutate state.pos/state.yaw/state.pitch -> DefaultCamera position/quaternion -> Player mirror position
```

### Event: Wave Clear

```text
updateEnemies() sees zero alive -> completeWave() -> unlockFragments() -> phase="upgrade" or victory
```

### Event: Upgrade Selection

```text
Input selection -> applyUpgrade(index) -> mutate weapon/player run state -> startNextWave()
```

## State Ownership Table

| Key / Store Path | Owner | Readers | Sync Mechanism | Reset On |
|------------------|-------|---------|----------------|----------|
| Local `state.phase` | `ma.machineArena` | Same behavior | Closure state | Run/menu transitions |
| Local `state.pos/yaw/pitch` | `ma.machineArena` | Same behavior | Closure state | `resetRun()` |
| Local `state.weapon` | `ma.machineArena` | Same behavior | Closure state | `resetRun()` |
| Local `enemies/projectiles/particles/trails` arrays | `ma.machineArena` | Same behavior | Closure arrays | `clearRuntimeActors()` / `cleanupRuntime()` |
| No `erth.store` keys | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A |

## Asset Pipeline

| Asset Key | Source | Format | Calibration Scale | Notes |
|-----------|--------|--------|-------------------|-------|
| `ProjectCover` | Generated | PNG | N/A | Bundled; thumbnail may be manual |
| `MA Menu Music` | `public/music1_alt.mp3` | MP3 | N/A | Loaded through `erth.asset.audio` |
| `MA Combat Music` | `public/music1.mp3` | MP3 | N/A | Loaded through `erth.asset.audio` |
| `MA Boss Music` | `public/music2.mp3` | MP3 | N/A | Loaded through `erth.asset.audio` |
| `MA Tyger Sound` | `public/tyger.mp3` | MP3 | N/A | One-shot victory/special audio |

## Init-Order Dependencies

```text
1. Stemscript creates GameRoot and Player.
2. Stemscript attaches ma.machineArena to GameRoot.
3. init() caches game, erth, input manager, renderer canvas, and Player.
4. init() builds runtime geometry and UIKit.
5. update() drives all gameplay once state.phase is playing.
```

## Physics Configuration

The first pass uses custom AABB collision in the behavior instead of engine physics bodies. Runtime world cover/walls populate an `obstacles` array. Elevated surfaces populate `walkables`.

| Object | Collision Type | Shape | Mass | Layer | Notes |
|--------|----------------|-------|------|-------|-------|
| Player | custom kinematic | capsule concept | 0 | default | Camera/body position with radius checks |
| Arena walls/cover | custom static | AABB | 0 | default | `obstacles` entries |
| Enemies | custom kinematic | radius | 0 | default | Separation and obstacle depenetration |

## Memory Budget

| Category | Count | Geometry | Materials | Textures | Notes |
|----------|-------|----------|-----------|----------|-------|
| Arena | ~100 meshes | Box/cylinder primitives | Node materials | none | Built once per run |
| Enemies | Wave dependent | Primitive groups | Node materials | none | Disposed on death/reset |
| VFX | Short-lived | Boxes/lines | Node materials | none | Disposed by lifetime |
| UI | One fullscreen | UIKit | Node materials | none | Disposed on behavior dispose |

## Current Bug Investigation Contract

- Movement must not depend on custom behavior inputs for core WASD/mouse movement.
- If custom inputs are unreliable in the runtime, menu/start actions need a fallback or default-binding path.
- Any input fallback must preserve validator cleanliness and be documented here.
- Runtime fallback: canvas click starts a run and requests pointer lock; default movement/action input can also deploy from the menu. Generated custom key inputs remain convenience bindings only.

## Open Technical Questions

- Whether behavior-declared `inputs:` are registered in the current StemStudio runtime for generated YAML behaviors attached through stemscript.
- Whether the current runtime requires `game.inputManager.resume()` after menu/UI states.

# Machine Arena Gameplay Design

## Core Fantasy

The player feels like a countermeasure dropped into a hostile machine arena, barely surviving waves long enough to turn a basic weapon into a runaway roguelite build.

## Game Loop

### Primary Loop

| Step | Player Action | System Response | Reward |
|------|---------------|-----------------|--------|
| 1 | Pick class and deploy | FPS arena starts, enemies spawn at arena edges | Immediate combat start |
| 2 | Move, aim, shoot, reload, use tactical tools | Enemies pursue, attack, telegraph, and drop gears on death | Score, gears, survival room |
| 3 | Clear the wave | Upgrade-card overlay appears | One run upgrade, ammo refresh, next wave |

### Secondary Loop

Run gears feed the workshop. Workshop upgrades unlock stronger starts, class choice, and fragment selection for later runs.

### Session Arc

The session starts in a menu/workshop state, escalates through 15 waves with boss spikes every fifth wave, and ends in victory or system failure with banked gears.

## Mechanic Rationale

| Mechanic | Why It Exists | What It Replaces If Removed | Risk If Poorly Tuned |
|----------|---------------|-----------------------------|----------------------|
| FPS movement and mouse look | Core boomer-shooter feel | Static turret combat | If broken, the game is unplayable |
| Weapon classes | Distinct starts and speed/health tradeoffs | One flat weapon | Classes can feel cosmetic |
| Upgrade cards | Roguelite power curve | Linear wave survival | Weak cards make waves repetitive |
| Tactical abilities | Emergency movement/defense choices | Pure DPS race | Cooldowns can trivialize or vanish |
| Boss waves | Session peaks and source identity | Flat horde mode | Bosses can feel unfair or irrelevant |
| Workshop | Between-run progression | One-session arcade loop | Persistence gaps reduce long-term motivation |

## State Machine

```text
[menu] --Enter--> [playing]
[menu] --Tab--> [workshop]
[workshop] --Enter--> [playing]
[playing] --wave clear--> [upgrade]
[upgrade] --1/2/3--> [playing]
[playing] --Tab--> [shop]
[shop] --Tab/Esc--> [playing]
[playing] --Esc--> [paused]
[paused] --Esc/Enter--> [playing]
[playing] --health <= 0--> [gameover]
[playing] --wave 15 clear--> [victory]
[gameover|victory] --Enter--> [menu]
```

## Difficulty Curve

| Phase | Duration | Challenge Level | New Elements Introduced |
|-------|----------|-----------------|------------------------|
| Early | Waves 1-4 | Low-Medium | Swarms, drones, walkers, upgrade ramp |
| Boss 1 | Wave 5 | Medium | Carmackion and jump pads |
| Mid | Waves 6-9 | Medium-High | Tanks, snipers, mortars, shielders |
| Boss 2 | Wave 10 | High | Darioltman and stronger specials |
| Late | Waves 11-14 | High | Spectres, fragments, dense mixed waves |
| Finale | Wave 15 | Very High | Nanoman |

## Tuning Parameters

| Parameter | Default | Min | Max | Affects | Tuning Note |
|-----------|---------|-----|-----|---------|-------------|
| `totalWaves` | 15 | 1 | 30 | Session length | Source-faithful default |
| `arenaSize` | 40 | 18 | 80 | Room scale and spawn distance | Larger arenas slow pressure |
| `baseFov` | 75 | 55 | 110 | Camera feel | FPS comfort |
| `mouseSensitivity` | 0.002 | 0.0005 | 0.01 | Aim feel | Critical to player feel |
| `gravity` | 22 | 5 | 60 | Jump arc | Source value |
| `jumpForce` | 9 | 3 | 25 | Jump height | Source value |
| `waveBaseCount` | 3 | 1 | 20 | Enemy count | Source curve base |
| `waveCountStep` | 2 | 0 | 8 | Enemy count growth | Source curve step |

## Reward Loops

| Trigger | Reward Type | Magnitude | Feedback |
|---------|-------------|-----------|----------|
| Enemy kill | Score and gears | Enemy-dependent | Particles, HUD update |
| Wave clear | Upgrade card | One choice | Upgrade overlay |
| Boss kill | Score/gears and progression | Large | Message, particles |
| Run end | Banked gears | Run total | Game over/victory overlay |

## Current Bug Fixes / Improvements

| Date | Issue | Design Impact |
|------|-------|---------------|
| 2026-04-29 | Player reports no movement after load | Movement/input is a blocking core-loop failure; startup and play-state input must work without relying on unsupported custom bindings. Click-to-start and default-action fallbacks are required. |

## Risk Register

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| InputManager custom action registration may not activate for generated behavior inputs | Start/menu/movement can appear dead | Use default InputManager actions where possible and keep start/play controls reachable |
| Monolithic behavior is large | Harder future iteration | Keep docs current; split later after runtime works |
| Boss choreography is approximate | Source-fidelity gap | Track as later polish |

## Open Questions

- After movement is restored, the player-feel gate still needs manual feedback on speed, mouse sensitivity, and jump arc.

# Machine Arena Conversion Plan

## Source Target

- Source repository: `https://github.com/EdgeTypE/machine-arena`
- Source commit inspected: `e96830644a5b997cca1628c21995139667048d76` (`2026-04-29`, `Update metaProgression.js`)
- Entry point: `index.html` loading Vite module `src/main.js`
- Canonical title: `Machine Arena`, derived from the user request, README heading, and `package.json` name.

## Genre And Classification

- 3D first-person arena roguelite / boomer shooter.
- Gameplay is true 3D: FPS camera, verticality, jump pads, elevated walkways, projectile hazards, and enemy pursuit in XZ space.

## Core Systems To Preserve

- Class loadout selection: LMG, rifle, shotgun.
- WASD/mouse FPS movement, unlockable jump, double jump, sprint, melee, grenades, and right-click tactical abilities.
- 15-wave arena structure with bosses on waves 5, 10, and 15.
- Upgrade-card selection after each completed wave.
- Field gear shop during combat via `Tab`.
- Workshop concept for persistent run upgrades and class selection.
- Motherboard fragment style passives as rare/unlockable modifiers.
- Industrial procedural arena with walls, crates, pillars, walkways, jump pads, hazard lighting, and phase color shifts.

## Asset Extraction Plan

- Copy source music files from `public/` into `machine-arena/audio/`.
- Copy one README screenshot into `textures/source-screens/` as provenance/reference.
- Generate square project cover art as `cover.png` and import it as `ProjectCover`.
- Source uses procedural Three.js geometry for gameplay objects, so no GLTF/GLB conversion is required.

## Mapping Strategy

- Use one custom behavior, `ma.machineArena`, for the tightly coupled FPS loop, procedural arena, enemy runtime, projectiles, upgrades, UI, and audio.
- Use StemStudio primitives only for stable host objects: `GameRoot`, invisible tagged `Player`, and the default camera.
- Build source-like runtime visuals as raw Three.js children of `GameRoot` and `DefaultCamera`, marked `userData.isRuntimeOnly = true`.
- Use UIKit for all HUD/menu overlays. No DOM overlay is ported.
- Use `game.inputManager` for default movement, mouse, fire, reload, melee, jump, sprint, and right-click. Keep raw key listeners only for non-default keys (`Digit1-3`, `Tab`, `KeyG`, `Escape`, `Enter`) with validator suppressions.

## Exposed Configuration Surface

- `totalWaves`: final mission wave count, default `15`.
- `arenaSize`: square arena half-size, default `40`.
- `baseFov`: normal camera field of view, default `75`.
- `mouseSensitivity`: pointer-lock look sensitivity, default `0.002`.
- `playerEyeHeight`: camera eye height above player capsule, default `1.6`.
- `gravity`: vertical gravity strength, default `22`.
- `jumpForce`: source jump force, default `9`.
- `waveBaseCount` and `waveCountStep`: source wave population curve, defaults `3` and `2`.
- `enemyHealthMultiplier`, `enemyDamageMultiplier`, `gearDropMultiplier`, `damageMultiplier`: balancing multipliers.
- `musicVolume` and `sfxVolume`: runtime audio levels.
- `debugLogs`: uniform opt-in debug logger.

## Risk Areas And Follow-Ups

- The original has thousands of lines of bespoke boss projectile patterns, DOM menus, generated WebAudio SFX, and localStorage meta-progression. The first StemStudio port keeps the playable loop and main mechanics but approximates the exact boss choreography, workshop 3D room, and persistent save semantics.
- The source uses a low-resolution render target for pixelation. StemStudio postprocessing does not directly expose that exact pass here; the port uses low-poly geometry, node materials, bloom, and HUD styling instead.
- Project thumbnail assignment is not set by stemscript. `ProjectCover` is bundled, but assigning it to discovery surfaces may remain an editor-side step.

# Machine Arena Source Map

## Entrypoints

| Source | Destination | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `index.html` | `machine-arena.stemscript` | StemStudio import script replaces Vite page bootstrap. |
| `src/main.js` | `behaviors/maMachineArena.yaml` | Main loop, state, FPS input, waves, combat, UI, shop, audio, and boss flow collapsed into one custom behavior. |

## Runtime Systems

| Source file/system | StemStudio mapping | Fidelity notes |
|---|---|---|
| `src/arena.js` | `buildArena()` inside `ma.machineArena` | Preserves industrial floor plates, octagonal bounds, pillars/crates, elevated walkways, jump pads, lighting shifts, and hazards with simplified geometry. |
| `src/weapons.js` | `WEAPON_DEFS` inside behavior | Source weapon damage, fire-rate, magazine, reload, range, spread, pellet count, recoil, health, and movement speed are carried over. |
| `src/enemies.js` | `ENEMY_DEFS`, enemy mesh builders, `spawnWave()`, `updateEnemies()` | Enemy roster, wave health scaling, boss waves, sniper telegraph, mortar zones, medic healing, shielder frontal mitigation, and boss phases are preserved in simplified form. |
| `src/upgrades.js` | `UPGRADES`, upgrade selection overlay | Card pool and core upgrade effects are preserved. Cards are selected with keys `1-3` instead of DOM click cards. |
| `src/fragments.js` | `FRAGMENTS`, fragment runtime helpers | Preserves the main fragment identities and effects in lower-complexity form: aura, spear, rocket, slow, damage streak, shell, overclock, memento, glass cannon, blood link. |
| `src/metaProgression.js` | In-memory `meta` object and workshop UI | Universal/class/ability upgrade concept is preserved. Browser `localStorage` persistence is intentionally not relied on for the first StemStudio pass. |
| `src/workshop3d.js` | `phase === "workshop"` UIKit overlay | Functional upgrade purchasing and class choice preserved; separate 3D lab room and terminal renderer are deferred. |
| `src/effects.js` | `spawnTrail()`, `spawnParticle()`, weapon view model | Preserves muzzle flash, projectile trails, hit particles, enemy death sparks, and simple first-person weapon meshes. |
| `public/*.mp3` | `audio/*.mp3` imports | Source music is bundled and resolved by behavior audio attributes. |
| `others/1.png` | `textures/source-screens/about-this-game.png` | Bundled as a reference/provenance screenshot, not used in gameplay. |

## Player Identification

The source player is the FPS camera/controller state in `src/main.js`. In StemStudio this maps to the invisible `Player` capsule with `tag=Player`, while the custom behavior drives `DefaultCamera` as the first-person view and mirrors the player position onto `Player`.

# Machine Arena - StemStudio Port

Machine Arena is a first-person arena roguelite port of `EdgeTypE/machine-arena`. The port keeps the 15-wave machine arena loop, class weapons, upgrade cards, tactical abilities, fragments, boss waves, field shop, and workshop progression concept inside a StemStudio project.

## Controls

- `Click canvas`, `Enter`, or any movement key: start run / continue from menus
- `1`, `2`, `3`: choose LMG, rifle, shotgun; select upgrade cards
- `WASD`: move
- `Mouse`: look / aim after pointer lock
- `Left click`: shoot
- `Right click`: tactical ability
- `R`: reload
- `F`: melee
- `Space`: jump after unlock
- `Shift`: sprint after unlock
- `G`: grenade after unlock
- `Tab`: field shop / workshop
- `Esc`: pause / close overlay

If the editor/runtime does not register generated custom key bindings, click the canvas to deploy. Core WASD/mouse movement uses StemStudio's default InputManager bindings.

## Objective

Clear 15 waves, choose one upgrade after each wave, survive boss waves 5, 10, and 15, and bank gears for workshop upgrades between runs.

## Architecture

- `machine-arena.stemscript` imports assets, creates `GameRoot`, creates a tagged invisible `Player`, disables the built-in camera controller, and attaches `ma.machineArena`.
- `behaviors/maMachineArena.yaml` owns gameplay: procedural arena, FPS camera, input, weapons, enemies, waves, bosses, upgrades, fragments, shop, UIKit HUD, and audio.
- Runtime meshes are visual-only children under `GameRoot` or `DefaultCamera` and are cleaned up by the behavior.
- Shared runtime state stays inside the behavior; no cross-behavior store contract is required for this first port.

## Assets

- Source music files are copied from `public/`.
- One source README screenshot is bundled for provenance.
- `cover.png` is generated cover art and imported as `ProjectCover`. Stemscript bundles it, but assigning it as the editor/project thumbnail may still be an editor-side action.

## Configuration

Tune the attached `MA Machine Arena` behavior for arena size, total waves, movement/camera feel, wave population, global difficulty multipliers, audio volume, and debug logging.

## Extension Points

- Split boss-specific attacks into separate helpers if exact source choreography is needed.
- Replace the simplified workshop overlay with a dedicated 3D workshop behavior.
- Add persistent save integration if StemStudio project policy allows browser storage or a service-backed profile.
- Add the source icon set to upgrade cards if card art becomes important for UI fidelity.

## Manual Validation

- Import `machine-arena.stemscript` in StemStudio.
- Confirm the cover asset appears in imported assets.
- Press `Enter`, choose a class, click the canvas, and confirm pointer lock.
- Clear wave 1, choose an upgrade with `1-3`, open the field shop with `Tab`, and verify restart/game-over flow.

# Machine Arena Conversion Notes

## Decision Log

- The source has one Vite/Three.js entrypoint and no authored model files, so the port uses a single custom behavior plus procedural runtime geometry.
- The source player is a camera-driven FPS body. The StemStudio scene includes a tagged invisible `Player` capsule for engine interoperability, while the behavior controls `DefaultCamera`.
- DOM overlays were replaced with UIKit so editor/play rendering stays inside StemStudio.
- Runtime-created meshes are attached under `GameRoot` or `DefaultCamera`, marked `userData.isRuntimeOnly = true`, and disposed by the behavior.
- WebGPU-safe `*NodeMaterial` constructors are used for custom geometry.

## Source-Faithful Vs Stem-Native Replacement

| Source feature | StemStudio result |
|---|---|
| Vite/DOM game bootstrap | `.stemscript` project import and one behavior. |
| DOM menus/cards/HUD | UIKit fullscreen HUD and keyboard state overlays. |
| Raw pointer lock and keys | InputManager defaults plus narrow raw listeners for unsupported keys. |
| Low-res render-target pixelation | Low-poly geometry, hard color bands, fog, and bloom. |
| Procedural arena geometry | Procedural runtime Three.js geometry. |
| Separate workshop renderer | Functional UIKit workshop state. |
| localStorage meta-progression | In-memory run/session meta-progression. |
| Generated WebAudio SFX | Source music assets plus visual feedback. |

## Gap Log

- Bosses preserve identity, phase thresholds, and signature attacks, but not every original callback and projectile pattern.
- The 3D workshop lab/corridor scene is deferred.
- Persistent browser save data is not written in this pass.
- The source has many generated SFX; this port bundles the music and uses visual feedback for most impacts.
- Stemscript imports `ProjectCover`, but editor-side project thumbnail assignment may still require manual assignment outside the script format.

## Manual Follow-Up

- Import `machine-arena.stemscript` in StemStudio and assign `ProjectCover` as the project thumbnail if the editor does not do that automatically.
- Play-test boss waves 5, 10, and 15 for feel; exact source projectile choreography is intentionally approximated.
- Decide whether the workshop should be upgraded from a functional UIKit overlay to a full 3D lab scene in a later pass.

## Tuning Surface

The behavior exposes wave count, arena size, FPS camera, movement, wave population, enemy/damage/gear multipliers, and audio levels. Weapon and enemy profiles remain internal because they are source identity constants, while global multipliers are exposed for balancing.

## Validation Checklist

- `ProjectCover` exists and is imported.
- `Player` exists and has `tag=Player`.
- `DefaultCamera` is set to `cameraType=NONE` with `near=0.1`.
- All imported asset files exist in the bundle.
- Custom behavior includes `debugLogs` and lifecycle logging.
- Final validators were run at handoff; see final response for exact results.

# Conversion Plan — Small World

## Source Target

- **Repo**: https://github.com/brunosimon/small-world
- **Author**: Bruno Simon
- **Framework**: Vanilla Three.js (r134) + GSAP + Webpack
- **Entry point**: `src/script.js` → `Experience.js` singleton

## Genre Classification

**Interactive Diorama / 3D Art Piece** — No gameplay, physics, or win conditions. The experience is pure observation and camera exploration of a hand-crafted miniature world scene.

## Core Systems

| System | Source Implementation | Conversion Strategy |
|--------|----------------------|---------------------|
| **Matcap Rendering** | Custom ShaderMaterial with matcap lookup + floor bounce + point light | Custom behavior with standalone GLSL shaders |
| **Wind Animation** | Vertex shader deformation using vertex color mask | Same approach, `USE_WIND` define on flag materials |
| **Telescope/Gears** | GSAP tweens, continuous random rotation | Procedural tweens replacing GSAP |
| **Floor Background** | Fullscreen clip-space quad with 4-corner vertex color gradient | Recreated as runtime Three.js mesh |
| **Floor Overlays** | Baked shadow/light meshes with alpha mask shaders | Same approach, custom ShaderMaterial |
| **Entrance Model** | White emissive mesh | Set emissive color on imported model |
| **Entrance Strokes** | 10-instance InstancedMesh with GSAP tweens | InstancedMesh with procedural animation |
| **Wind Strokes** | Randomly spawned cloned meshes with progress shader | Same approach, procedural animation |
| **Camera** | Spherical orbit with smooth easing, boom/truck, clamped ranges | Custom camera behavior |
| **Depth of Field** | BokehPass with raycasted dynamic focus | **Not ported** — Stem has no DOF pass |

## Asset Extraction Plan

### Models (6 GLBs, DRACO-compressed)
- `model.glb` — Main diorama (all named meshes: telescopeY/X, gear0/1, flag*)
- `entranceModel.glb` — Entrance portal
- `bakedFloorShadowModel.glb` — Floor shadow overlay mesh
- `bakedFloorLightModel.glb` — Floor light overlay mesh
- `windStroke1Model.glb` — Wind stroke template variant 1
- `windStroke2Model.glb` — Wind stroke template variant 2

### Textures (14 PNGs)
- 11 matcap textures (brass, fabricPinkDark/Light, foliage, globe, lens, metal, paper, stone, stoneDark, wood)
- 2 floor baked textures (shadow alpha, light alpha)
- 1 entrance stroke alpha map

## Built-in Reuse

| Built-in | Used? | Reason |
|----------|-------|--------|
| `rotation` lambda | No | Telescope needs GSAP-like random motion, not constant spin |
| `animation` behavior | No | No skeletal animations in source |
| `genericSound` | No | No audio in source |
| `tween` behavior | No | Custom shader-driven animations not compatible |

## Custom Systems

| Custom System | Purpose |
|---------------|---------|
| `sw.smallWorld` behavior | All visual content, shaders, animations |
| `sw.orbitCamera` behavior | Spherical orbit camera navigation |

## Expected Manual Follow-ups

1. Verify DRACO-compressed GLBs load correctly in Script Tool
2. Verify matcap texture names match model material names
3. Tune matcap shader appearance if Three.js version differences affect color space

## Open Questions / Risk Areas

- **DRACO decoding**: Source GLBs are DRACO-compressed; StemStudio's GLTFLoader should handle this but worth verifying
- **Three.js version delta**: Source uses r134, Stem uses 0.179. The custom matcap shader avoids Three.js includes to prevent compatibility issues
- **Color space**: Matcap textures may render slightly differently due to color management changes between r134 and r179
- **Depth of field**: Omitted entirely — this is a presentation-layer approximation documented in conversion-notes

# Small World — StemStudio Conversion

**Source**: [brunosimon/small-world](https://github.com/brunosimon/small-world)
**Genre**: Interactive Diorama / 3D Art Piece
**Author**: Bruno Simon

## Overview

A hand-crafted miniature world diorama viewed through a smooth spherical orbit camera. The scene features custom matcap shading with floor bounce and point lighting effects, an animated telescope with mechanically-linked gears, a glowing entrance portal with animated stroke particles, and ambient wind stroke VFX.

There is no gameplay — the experience is pure observation and camera exploration.

## Architecture

### Behaviors

| Behavior | ID | Purpose |
|----------|-------|---------|
| SW Small World | `sw.smallWorld` | All visual content: matcap material replacement, floor background/overlays, entrance model + strokes, wind strokes, telescope/gear animation |
| SW Orbit Camera | `sw.orbitCamera` | Spherical orbit camera with smooth easing, zoom, and boom/truck pan |

### Data Flow

```
stemscript
  ├── imports assets (11 matcaps, 3 textures, 6 models)
  ├── imports behaviors (2)
  ├── configures scene settings (background, lighting, tonemapping)
  └── attaches both behaviors to SWDiorama model

swSmallWorld.init()
  ├── loads matcap textures via erth.asset.image
  ├── traverses model, replaces MeshStandardMaterial → custom matcap ShaderMaterial
  ├── creates camera-locked floor background gradient quad
  ├── sets up floor shadow/light overlays (custom alpha mask shader)
  ├── configures entrance model (white emissive)
  ├── creates entrance strokes (InstancedMesh, 10 instances)
  └── locates wind stroke templates (hidden in scene)

swSmallWorld.update(dt)
  ├── updates shader uTime uniform (flag wind animation)
  ├── advances telescope/gear procedural tweens
  ├── updates entrance stroke instance matrices
  └── manages wind stroke lifecycle (spawn/animate/dispose)

swOrbitCamera.init()
  ├── configures camera (FOV 25, near 0.1, far 150)
  ├── initializes spherical coordinates
  └── attaches mouse/touch/wheel event listeners to the renderer canvas

swOrbitCamera.update(dt)
  ├── processes input deltas → spherical target
  ├── eases current values toward targets
  ├── converts spherical → cartesian camera position
  └── applies boom/truck offset in camera-local space
```

## Asset Provenance

All assets are original files from the source repository:

| Asset | Source Path | Destination |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| Main diorama model | `static/assets/model.glb` | `models/model.glb` |
| Entrance model | `static/assets/entranceModel.glb` | `models/entranceModel.glb` |
| Floor shadow mesh | `static/assets/bakedFloorShadowModel.glb` | `models/bakedFloorShadowModel.glb` |
| Floor light mesh | `static/assets/bakedFloorLightModel.glb` | `models/bakedFloorLightModel.glb` |
| Wind stroke 1 | `static/assets/windStroke1Model.glb` | `models/windStroke1Model.glb` |
| Wind stroke 2 | `static/assets/windStroke2Model.glb` | `models/windStroke2Model.glb` |
| 11 matcap textures | `static/assets/matcaps/*.png` | `textures/matcaps/*.png` |
| Floor shadow texture | `static/assets/bakedFloorShadowTexture.png` | `textures/bakedFloorShadowTexture.png` |
| Floor light texture | `static/assets/bakedFloorLightTexture.png` | `textures/bakedFloorLightTexture.png` |
| Entrance stroke texture | `static/assets/entranceStrokeTexture.png` | `textures/entranceStrokeTexture.png` |

## Controls

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| Left-click drag | Orbit camera (rotate around scene) |
| Right-click / Shift+click / Ctrl+click drag | Boom/truck (lateral pan) |
| Mouse wheel | Zoom (change orbit radius) |
| Single-finger drag (touch) | Orbit camera |
| Two-finger drag (touch) | Boom/truck |
| Pinch (touch) | Zoom |

Camera rotation is clamped to prevent viewing from below or from extreme angles. Zoom is clamped between radius 9 and 30.

## What the Script Creates

1. Imports 14 textures and 6 GLB models
2. Imports 2 custom behaviors
3. Sets scene background, lighting, and tone mapping
4. Disables shadows (matcap materials are self-lit)
5. Configures camera for diorama viewing
6. Attaches both behaviors to the main model

## Extension Points

- **Colors**: Modify `sharedUniforms` in `swSmallWorld.yaml` to change floor bounce color, point light color, or positions
- **Camera range**: Adjust `spherical.limits` in `swOrbitCamera.yaml` to change orbit constraints
- **Wind intensity**: Modify the wind shader multiplier (`0.12`) in the matcap vertex shader
- **Entrance strokes**: Change `strokeCount`, `strokeLength`, or animation timing in the behavior
- **Wind strokes**: Adjust spawn rate (`0.3` probability), duration (`10s`), or drift distance (`1.5`)

- [ ] Green floor gradient background visible

- [ ] Entrance portal glows white

# Conversion Notes — Small World

## Decision Log

| # | Decision | Rationale |
|---|----------|-----------|
| 1 | Write standalone GLSL shaders instead of using Three.js `#include` directives | Source uses r134 includes; Stem uses r179 where includes have changed (e.g., `matcapTexelToLinear` removed). Standalone shaders are version-portable. |
| 2 | Replace GSAP with procedural tweening | StemStudio does not bundle GSAP. Procedural easing (quadratic, quartic) matches GSAP's `power2.inOut`, `power2.in`, `power4.in` curves. |
| 3 | Omit depth of field (BokehPass) | StemStudio postprocessing supports AO, Bloom, Outline but not DOF. This is purely visual polish. |
| 4 | Use `renderOrder = -1000` on background quad | Ensures the gradient quad renders before all scene objects, matching the source's clip-space background behavior. |
| 5 | Use single behavior for all world content | All visual systems are tightly coupled (shared uniforms, scene references). Splitting would add complexity without benefit. |
| 6 | Separate camera into its own behavior | Camera orbit is independently configurable and potentially reusable. Clean separation of concerns. |
| 7 | Skip `normalizeWheel` dependency | Modern browsers provide consistent `deltaY` values. Raw `event.deltaY` with the source's speed factor gives equivalent feel. |
| 8 | Anchor the background quad to the active camera | The source floor background is effectively screen-space. Attaching the gradient quad to the camera preserves that look inside the engine instead of leaving it as a world-space plane. |
| 9 | Clone wind-stroke geometry per spawned instance | The hidden template meshes remain in the scene for reuse, so spawned strokes must not dispose shared template geometry on completion. |

## Gap Log

| Feature | Status | Impact |
|---------|--------|--------|
| BokehPass (DOF) | **Not ported** | Tilt-shift miniature effect is lost. The scene still looks correct but lacks the signature shallow-depth-of-field aesthetic. |
| Debug panel (Tweakpane) | **Not ported** | Debug-only feature, not part of the experience. |
| Stats panel | **Not ported** | Debug-only performance monitor. |
| OrbitControls debug camera | **Not ported** | Debug-only alternative camera mode. |
| `matcapTexelToLinear()` | **Approximated** | Source converts matcap texture from sRGB to linear before lighting, then back to sRGB. Standalone shader works in the texture's native space. Colors may differ very slightly. |
| `encodings_fragment` output | **Approximated** | Shader outputs directly without Three.js' automatic encoding conversion. |
| `mergeUniforms` with UniformsLib | **Replaced** | Source merges with `THREE.UniformsLib.common`, etc. Standalone shader declares only needed uniforms. |

## Manual Follow-ups

1. **Verify DRACO decoding** — Confirm `model.glb` and other GLBs load in the Script Tool. StemStudio should handle DRACO automatically.
2. **Check matcap material matching** — The model's mesh materials must have names matching the MATCAP_MAP keys (brass, fabricPinkDark, etc.). If names differ, update the map in the behavior.
3. **Tune colors if needed** — If matcap colors appear washed out or over-saturated due to color space differences, consider adding `pow(texColor, vec3(2.2))` linearization in the fragment shader and `pow(outgoing, vec3(1.0/2.2))` output encoding.
4. **Entrance stroke texture** — Verify `entranceStrokeTexture.png` is loaded with correct `flipY` for the instanced plane geometry.

## Source-Faithful vs Stem-Native Replacement Table

| Feature | Source | Stem Conversion | Faithful? |
|---------|--------|-----------------|-----------|
| Matcap materials | Custom ShaderMaterial | Custom ShaderMaterial | Yes (standalone GLSL) |
| Floor bounce lighting | Fragment shader uniform mix | Same approach | Yes |
| Point light effect | Fragment shader additive blend | Same approach | Yes |
| Flag wind deformation | Vertex shader sin() displacement | Same approach | Yes |
| Telescope animation | GSAP power2.inOut tweens | Procedural easeInOutQuad | Functionally equivalent |
| Gear rotation | Direct ratio from telescope.y | Same approach | Yes |
| Floor background | Clip-space gradient quad | Same approach | Yes |
| Floor overlays | ShaderMaterial with alpha mask | Same approach | Yes |
| Entrance emissive | MeshStandardMaterial.emissive | Same approach | Yes |
| Entrance strokes | InstancedMesh + GSAP tweens | InstancedMesh + procedural | Functionally equivalent |
| Wind strokes | Clone + GSAP progress/position | Clone + procedural | Functionally equivalent |
| Camera orbit | Custom spherical + smooth easing | Same approach | Yes |
| Boom/truck pan | Camera-local translateX/Y | Same approach | Yes |
| Depth of field | BokehPass + raycasted focus | **Omitted** | No |
| Cursor feedback | grab/grabbing CSS cursor | Same approach | Yes |

## Mapping Rationale

The source is a self-contained diorama with no external dependencies beyond Three.js and GSAP. All rendering is custom (matcap shaders, baked overlays, clip-space background). The conversion preserves this by using the same custom shader approach within StemStudio behaviors.

The only significant omission is the BokehPass depth of field, which contributes to the tilt-shift miniature photography aesthetic but does not affect interactivity or content comprehension.

## Updated Documentation Compliance (v2)

### Changes Made

#### swSmallWorld.yaml

**P0: Added `attributes` section to config**
- The config was missing the required `attributes` key entirely. Added three `imageAsset` attributes:
  - `floorShadowTexture` — Baked shadow alpha mask for floor overlay
  - `floorLightTexture` — Baked light alpha mask for floor overlay
  - `entranceStrokeTexture` — Alpha map for entrance stroke instances

**P1: Converted hardcoded asset references to compliant patterns**
- **Matcap textures (11 textures loaded in a loop via MATCAP_MAP):** Converted from `this.erth.asset.image.createTexture({ name: "..." })` to `this.erth.asset.image.findByName(name)` followed by `this.erth.asset.image.createTexture(ref)`. Used `findByName` because the set of 11 matcap textures is dynamic and iterated from a map, making individual attributes impractical. Added null-check for the returned ref.
- **Floor shadow texture (`SWFloorShadowTex`):** Converted from `this.erth.asset.image.createTexture({ name: "SWFloorShadowTex" })` to attribute-first pattern: reads `this.attributes.floorShadowTexture`, falls back to `this.erth.asset.image.findByName("SWFloorShadowTex")`, then passes the resolved ref to `createTexture(ref)`.
- **Floor light texture (`SWFloorLightTex`):** Same pattern as floor shadow. Reads `this.attributes.floorLightTexture`, falls back to `findByName("SWFloorLightTex")`.
- **Entrance stroke texture (`SWEntranceStrokeTex`):** Same pattern. Reads `this.attributes.entranceStrokeTexture`, falls back to `findByName("SWEntranceStrokeTex")`.
- **`this.init` made `async`** to support `await` on `findByName()` and `createTexture()` calls, which return Promises.

#### swOrbitCamera.yaml

**P0: Already compliant** — `attributes` section was already present with `fov` (number) and `centerY` (number).

**P1: No changes needed** — No `erth.asset.*` calls exist in this behavior. It is a pure camera controller with no asset loading.

### Summary of Violations Fixed

| File | Issue | Fix Applied |
|------|-------|-------------|
| swSmallWorld.yaml | Missing `config.attributes` | Added `attributes` with 3 `imageAsset` entries |
| swSmallWorld.yaml | `createTexture({ name: "MCBrass" })` etc. (11 matcap textures) | `findByName()` then `createTexture(ref)` |
| swSmallWorld.yaml | `createTexture({ name: "SWFloorShadowTex" })` | Attribute `floorShadowTexture` with `findByName` fallback |
| swSmallWorld.yaml | `createTexture({ name: "SWFloorLightTex" })` | Attribute `floorLightTexture` with `findByName` fallback |
| swSmallWorld.yaml | `createTexture({ name: "SWEntranceStrokeTex" })` | Attribute `entranceStrokeTexture` with `findByName` fallback |
| swSmallWorld.yaml | `this.init` was synchronous | Made `async` to support `await` on asset loading Promises |
| swOrbitCamera.yaml | (none) | Already compliant — no changes required |

nodes.content:
# Sky Bomber Conversion Plan

## Source Target

- Source repository: `https://github.com/skmail/bomber`
- Source commit inspected: `04dc865ce76ab78a3b845dd23299aeb4e6ca72a9`
- Entrypoint: `index.html` -> `src/main.tsx` -> `src/App.tsx` -> `src/Scene.tsx`
- Canonical title: `Sky Bomber`, derived from the user target `sky-bomber` and source title screen text `SKY BOMBER`.

## Genre And Classification

- 3D Bomberman-style action maze on procedural floating voxel islands.
- Gameplay is grid/tile based in XZ with discrete Y changes over bridges and islands.
- The player object is `Character` in `src/voxel/Character.tsx`; the StemStudio script creates a tagged invisible `Player` object and a runtime cuboid visual.

## Core Systems To Preserve

- Start, playing, cleared, and dead phases.
- Four procedural floating islands with bridge connections.
- Hard pillars, destructible crates, and treasure chests.
- WASD / joystick grid movement, with camera-relative directional intent.
- Space / touch button bomb placement.
- Classic plus blasts and mega plus+diagonal blasts.
- Enemy wander/chase behavior with FOV, LOS, and last-seen memory.
- Extra-bomb and mega-bomb pickups.
- Level progression with enemy count, speed, detection range, and seed changes.
- HUD/menu with level, bomb capacity, mega charges, enemy count, tile label, and phase overlays.

## Asset Plan

- Do not copy source OBJ/MTL/block-atlas assets into this bundle because the inspected repo has no explicit license file.
- Rebuild visuals procedurally with WebGPU-safe NodeMaterials and primitive geometry.
- Generate and bundle a square `cover.png`; import as `ProjectCover`.
- Keep `cover.svg` as the editable local source for the generated PNG.

## Mapping Strategy

- `sky-bomber.stemscript` creates the project, scene lighting, camera, runtime host, invisible tagged `Player`, touch controls, and attaches `sb.skyBomber`.
- `behaviors/sbSkyBomber.yaml` stays intentionally thin: lifecycle, debug logging, imports, update tick, editor preview hooks.
- `imports/sky-bomber-runtime.js` owns the custom procedural gameplay runtime.
- `imports/uikit-dual-mode.yaml` renders the same UIKit HUD/menu tree in play and editor preview.
- Runtime-created meshes are visual-only children under `SkyBomber.Root`, marked `userData.isRuntimeOnly = true`, and cleaned up in `dispose()`.

## Exposed Configuration Surface

| Attribute | Source Default | Purpose |
|---|---:|---|
| `seed` | 552 | Base world seed. |
| `islandSize` | 24 | Main island footprint size. |
| `mainDensity` | 0.15 | Main-island soft crate density. |
| `secondaryDensity` | 0.10 | Secondary-island soft crate density. |
| `bridgeArc` | -0.1 | Bridge sag/arc per tile. |
| `playerSpeed` | 6 | Tile-step interpolation speed. |
| `baseEnemyCount` | 3 | Level 1 enemy count before scaling. |
| `enemySpeed` | 1.8 | Level 1 enemy speed. |
| `maxBombs` | 3 | Base simultaneous bomb limit. |
| `fuseSeconds` | 2.0 | Bomb fuse. |
| `blastDuration` | 0.45 | Blast visibility/damage duration. |
| `cameraDistance` | 32 | Follow camera distance. |
| `cameraPitch` | 35 | Follow camera pitch. |
| `cameraYaw` | 135 | Follow camera yaw. |
| `cameraStiffness` | 5 | Follow camera smoothing. |
| `debugLogs` | false | Opt-in lifecycle and major gameplay logging. |

## Risk Areas And Follow-Ups

- Full source river carving, waterfall meshes, toon fog, palette/noise post-processing, and block OBJ visuals are approximated by Stem-native primitives, fog, bloom, clouds, and procedural island blocks.
- Source mobile UI is replaced by the built-in `touchControls` behavior plus UIKit phase overlays.
- Stemscript imports `ProjectCover`, but editor-side project thumbnail assignment may still require manual selection.

# Sky Bomber Source Map

## Entrypoints

| Source | StemStudio Mapping |
|---|---|
| `index.html`, `src/main.tsx`, `src/App.tsx` | `sky-bomber.stemscript` project bootstrap and behavior attach. |
| `src/Scene.tsx` | `sb.skyBomber` behavior lifecycle plus `sky-bomber-runtime` phase/world loop. |

## Runtime Systems

| Source File(s) | Source Role | Generated File/System |
|---|---|---|
| `src/voxel/world.ts`, `build.ts`, `pipeline.ts`, `generate.ts` | Four procedural floating islands, seed hashing, island placement. | `imports/sky-bomber-runtime.js` `buildPlacements`, `addIslandCells`. |
| `src/voxel/bridge.ts`, `Connectors.tsx` | Bridge layout, visual Y arc, walkable Y. | `buildBridge` with configurable `bridgeArc`. |
| `src/voxel/worldGrid.ts` | Packed gameplay flags for walkable/obstacle/entity/blast cells. | Runtime grid object with equivalent `F_*` flags and `gridHasColumn`. |
| `src/voxel/bomberman.ts` | Hard pillar lattice, soft crate densities, spawn clear radius. | `addObstacles`; main soft crates use a reachability filter before acceptance. |
| `src/voxel/Character.tsx`, `controls/*` | Player grid stepping, camera-relative controls, death checks. | `updatePlayer`, `desiredStep`, `pickNeighbor`, `checkPlayerDeath`. |
| `src/voxel/FollowCamera.tsx` | Smooth follow target/camera offset. | `updateCamera`, driven by `cameraDistance`, `cameraPitch`, `cameraYaw`, `cameraStiffness`. |
| `src/voxel/Bombs.tsx` | Fuse, blast propagation, soft destruction, mega bombs. | `placeBomb`, `updateBombs`, `detonate`, `destroySoft`. |
| `src/voxel/Enemies.tsx`, `Enemy.tsx` | Enemy spawn distribution, wander/chase AI, FOV, LOS, memory. | `createEnemies`, `enemyChaseState`, `hasLineOfSight`, `wanderStep`, `chaseStep`. |
| `src/voxel/Pickups.tsx` | Extra bomb and mega bomb pickups. | `spawnPickup`, `updatePickups`, `bombBonus`, `megaCharges`. |
| `src/MenuOverlay.tsx`, inline Scene HUD | Title/phase overlays and HUD readouts. | UIKit tree in `buildUi` and `updateUi`. |
| `src/MobileControls.tsx` | Source DOM mobile joystick + bomb button. | StemStudio scene-level `touchControls` behavior attached to `"Default Scene"` as required by bundle validation. |
| `src/voxel/CloudPuffs.tsx`, `CloudFloor.tsx`, `Skybox.tsx` | Atmosphere and clouds. | Stemscript gradient/fog plus runtime cloud puffs. |
| `src/voxel/RiverMesh.tsx`, `Waterfall.tsx`, `depthEffects.tsx` | Rivers, waterfalls, toon fog/post effects. | Visual approximation via terrain colors, fog, bloom, clouds; see fidelity decision record. |

## Asset Map

| Source Asset | Decision |
|---|---|
| `public/assets/models/*.obj`, `*.mtl`, `block_bits_texture.png` | Not copied; no explicit source license was present. Rebuilt procedurally with primitives. |
| `public/assets/block_atlas.png` | Not copied; the runtime uses NodeMaterial colors. |
| `public/favicon.svg` | Used only as source-reference inspiration for the generated project cover. |
| `cover.svg`, `cover.png` | Programmatically generated cover art bundled as `ProjectCover`. |

## Tuning Map

| Source Value | Source Location | Generated Attribute |
|---|---|---|
| `seed: 552` | `Scene.tsx useControls` | `seed` |
| `size: 24` | `Scene.tsx useControls` | `islandSize` |
| `mainDensity: 0.15` | `Scene.tsx useControls` | `mainDensity` |
| `secondaryDensity: 0.10` | `Scene.tsx useControls` | `secondaryDensity` |
| `bridgeArc: -0.1` | `Scene.tsx useControls` | `bridgeArc` |
| `PLAYER_SPEED = 6` | `Scene.tsx` | `playerSpeed` |
| `enemyCount = 3 + level - 1` | `Scene.tsx` | `baseEnemyCount` |
| `enemySpeed = 1.8 + level * 0.2` | `Scene.tsx` | `enemySpeed` |
| `maxBombs = 3` | `Scene.tsx useControls` | `maxBombs` |
| `FUSE_SECONDS = 2.0` | `Bombs.tsx` | `fuseSeconds` |
| `BLAST_DURATION = 0.45` | `Bombs.tsx` | `blastDuration` |
| `CAM_DISTANCE = 32`, `CAM_PITCH = 35`, `CAM_INITIAL_AZIMUTH = 135deg`, `FOLLOW_STIFFNESS = 5` | `Scene.tsx` | `cameraDistance`, `cameraPitch`, `cameraYaw`, `cameraStiffness` |

# Sky Bomber

StemStudio conversion of `https://github.com/skmail/bomber`: a Bomberman-style game on procedural floating voxel islands.

## What The Script Creates

- `SkyBomber.Root` runtime host.
- Invisible tagged `Player` capsule for engine interoperability.
- Custom `sb.skyBomber` behavior.
- `uikit-dual-mode` and `sky-bomber-runtime` script imports.
- Built-in `touchControls` for mobile/tablet joystick plus bomb button.
- `ProjectCover` image import from `cover.png`.

## Controls

- Desktop: WASD / arrow keys to move, Space to place a bomb, Enter to start/advance/restart.
- Mobile/tablet: left joystick to move, right button to place a bomb, tap phase overlays to start/advance/restart.

## Architecture

`behaviors/sbSkyBomber.yaml` is a small lifecycle wrapper. It imports:

- `imports/uikit-dual-mode.yaml` for editor/play UIKit mounting.
- `imports/sky-bomber-runtime.js` for procedural islands, grid flags, player stepping, bombs, enemies, pickups, camera follow, and HUD/menu state.

Gameplay state is local to the behavior on `owner._sb`. No `erth.store` keys are used.

## Configuration

Tune the attached behavior attributes for seed, island size, crate densities, bridge arc, player speed, enemy count/speed, bomb fuse/duration, max bombs, and camera distance/pitch/yaw/stiffness. `debugLogs` enables opt-in lifecycle and major gameplay logging.

## Asset Provenance

- `cover.svg` and `cover.png` were generated for this port.
- Source OBJ/MTL/image assets were not bundled because the inspected repository did not include a license file.
- All runtime game visuals are procedural primitives using WebGPU-safe NodeMaterials.

## Extension Points

- Add licensed GLB/texture assets to replace primitive blocks.
- Port rivers/waterfalls and toon fog if visual fidelity becomes the priority.
- Add audio feedback for bombs, pickups, and enemy deaths.
- Split the runtime import into smaller scripts if the game grows beyond this first-pass conversion.

## Manual Validation

- Import `sky-bomber.stemscript`.
- Verify the start overlay appears, then Enter/tap starts play.
- Move across land and bridges, place bombs, destroy crates/chests, collect pickups, kill all enemies, and advance to the next level.
- Trigger death by standing in a blast or touching an enemy, then restart.
- Assign `ProjectCover` as the project thumbnail if the editor does not do it automatically.

# Sky Bomber Conversion Notes

## Decision Log

- Source mode is Conversion: one Vite/R3F entrypoint, `src/Scene.tsx`, with gameplay split across `src/voxel/*`.
- The source player is `Character`; the port creates an invisible tagged `Player` object and a runtime-only cuboid visual controlled by `sb.skyBomber`.
- DOM/R3F UI was rebuilt as UIKit using the dual-mode helper.
- Raw keyboard/touch input was normalized to `game.inputManager` plus built-in `touchControls`.
- Source block/model assets were not copied because no license file was present. The port uses procedural primitives and NodeMaterials.
- Runtime-created objects are visual-only children under `SkyBomber.Root` and are marked `userData.isRuntimeOnly = true`.

## Source-Faithful Vs Stem-Native Replacement

| Source Feature | StemStudio Result |
|---|---|
| React/R3F scene graph | Stemscript host objects plus runtime Three.js children. |
| DOM title/HUD/mobile overlays | UIKit HUD/menu and `touchControls`. |
| Dense `WorldGrid` typed array | Behavior-local grid object with equivalent `F_*` flags. |
| OBJ/MTL block models and atlas | Procedural primitive blocks with NodeMaterials. |
| OrbitControls follow | Fixed smooth follow camera with exposed yaw/pitch/distance/stiffness. |
| River/waterfall/post stack | Gradient, fog, bloom, clouds, and colored island blocks. |
| Leva debug controls | Behavior attributes. |

## Gap Log

- Rivers, waterfalls, toon fog bands, palette shift, depth noise, and exact vignette/brightness chain are approximated.
- The source's exact block model geometry/atlas styling is not bundled.
- The source has no audio; the port does not add new audio.
- The source camera allows free orbit/zoom; the port uses a stable fixed follow view.
- Stemscript imports `ProjectCover`, but project thumbnail assignment may still require manual editor-side selection.

## Manual Follow-Up

- Import `sky-bomber.stemscript` in StemStudio and assign `ProjectCover` as the project thumbnail if the editor does not do that automatically.
- Play-test the first three levels for movement feel, enemy detection range, and bridge traversal.
- Decide whether to add exact river/waterfall rendering once the core loop is accepted.

## Tuning Surface

The behavior exposes source-derived island, obstacle, enemy, bomb, movement, camera, and debug parameters. Internal constants retained as source identity include hard pillar spacing, spawn clear radius, treasure density, pickup probabilities, enemy FOV half-angle, and enemy memory duration.

## Technical Validation Checklist

- `ProjectCover` exists as `cover.png` and is imported in the stemscript.
- `Player` exists and has `tag=Player`.
- `DefaultCamera` uses `cameraType=NONE` and `near=0.1`.
- UIKit uses `new UIKit.Fullscreen(renderer, ...)` through the dual-mode helper.
- Custom geometry uses WebGPU-safe `*NodeMaterial` classes.
- Runtime-only objects are marked and disposed.
- Final validators were run at handoff; see final response for exact results.

## Knowledge Sources Used

- `docs/workflows/analyze-source.md`
- `docs/workflows/gates.md`
- `docs/workflows/write-stemscript.md`
- `docs/workflows/verify-and-document.md`
- `docs/domains/yaml-behaviors.md`
- `docs/domains/ui-uikit-guide.md`
- `docs/domains/input-manager-guide.md`
- `docs/domains/touch-controls.md`
- `docs/domains/script-imports.md`

# Sky Bomber Mechanic Mapping

## Source Inventory

| Source System | Source File(s) | Type | Priority |
|---|---|---|---:|
| Phase/progression loop | `src/Scene.tsx`, `MenuOverlay.tsx` | Core | 10 |
| Procedural islands | `world.ts`, `build.ts`, `pipeline.ts`, `generate.ts` | Core | 9 |
| WorldGrid flags | `worldGrid.ts` | Core | 10 |
| Player movement | `Character.tsx`, `controls/*` | Core | 10 |
| Bombs and blasts | `Bombs.tsx` | Core | 10 |
| Enemy AI | `Enemies.tsx`, `Enemy.tsx` | Core | 9 |
| Obstacles/treasure | `bomberman.ts`, `Obstacles.tsx`, `Treasures.tsx` | Core | 9 |
| Pickups | `Pickups.tsx` | Core | 8 |
| HUD/menu | `Scene.tsx`, `MenuOverlay.tsx` | Core | 8 |
| Clouds/sky/post | `Skybox.tsx`, `CloudPuffs.tsx`, `depthEffects.tsx` | Polish | 5 |

## Built-In Coverage Assessment

| Source System | Built-In Candidate | Coverage | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mobile controls | `touchControls` | High for joystick + bomb button | Use built-in. |
| Camera | Built-in third-person camera | Medium; source needs custom fixed yaw/pitch smoothing | Custom camera in runtime. |
| UI | UIKit | High for HUD/menu content, not exact CSS animation | Use UIKit. |
| Physics/collision | Scene physics | Low; source uses discrete grid flags | Custom grid runtime. |
| World generation | Terrain/primitive commands | Low; source is procedural per level | Custom runtime geometry. |

## Custom Systems Needed

| Custom System | Type | Reason Built-In Insufficient | Complexity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sky-bomber-runtime` | script import | Tightly coupled grid, world, bombs, enemy, pickup, and camera logic. | High |
| `sb.skyBomber` | behavior | Lifecycle, debug logging, UIKit/editor/play integration. | Low |

## State Flow

- State is behavior-local on `owner._sb`.
- No `erth.store` keys are used.
- The hidden `Player` object is tagged `Player`; runtime mirrors its transform for engine systems.
- UIKit click handlers call `advance(owner)` directly inside the runtime import.
- `touchControls` feeds `game.inputManager`; the runtime reads `forward`, `lateral`, optional `move`, `jump`, and `sb_Enter`.

## Fidelity Risks

| Risk | Source Behavior | Stem Approximation | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| River/waterfall rendering | Source carves rivers, renders river meshes and waterfall ribbons | Not reproduced in this pass | Visual medium, gameplay low | Keep island/bridge/bomber loop intact; document in FDR. |
| Source OBJ block look | Source can load voxel model assets | Primitive NodeMaterial blocks | Visual medium | Avoid asset redistribution without license; keep chunky voxel readability. |
| Free orbit camera | OrbitControls can rotate and zoom | Fixed smooth follow camera | Feel low/medium | Expose yaw, pitch, distance, stiffness attributes. |

## Configuration Surface Plan

See `conversion-plan.md` for the full attribute table.

# Sky Bomber Fidelity Decision Record

## FDR-001: Procedural Primitive Visuals Instead Of Source OBJ/Atlas Assets

**Date:** 2026-04-29
**Status:** Accepted
**Impact:** Medium

### Context

The source repo includes OBJ/MTL block models, `block_bits_texture.png`, and `block_atlas.png` under `public/assets/`.

### Problem

The inspected source repository did not include a license file. Copying and redistributing source assets into a generated StemStudio bundle would be ambiguous.

### StemStudio Limitation

StemStudio can import images and models, but it cannot resolve third-party asset licensing. The conversion workflow should not bundle uncertain assets when a procedural alternative preserves gameplay.

### Gameplay Impact

The visual style is less source-exact, but core gameplay is unaffected.

### Options

#### Option A: Best Fidelity
Copy and convert source models/textures. Highest visual similarity, unclear redistribution rights.

#### Option B: Best Stem-Native
Use runtime-only primitive voxel meshes with WebGPU-safe NodeMaterials. Lower asset fidelity, clear ownership.

#### Option C: Fastest Fallback
Use only stemscript primitives. Fast, but level rebuilds and destructible state would be harder to manage.

### Decision

Option B. The port rebuilds the source look procedurally with colored blocks, crates, pillars, chests, bombs, pickups, clouds, and a cuboid player.

### Consequences

- Source gameplay remains portable without uncertain asset redistribution.
- Exact block atlas styling is not reproduced.
- Later work can swap primitives for licensed GLB assets.

### Files Affected

- `imports/sky-bomber-runtime.js`
- `sky-bomber.stemscript`

## FDR-002: Rivers, Waterfalls, And Source Post Effects Are Approximated

**Date:** 2026-04-29
**Status:** Accepted
**Impact:** Medium

### Context

The source includes river carving, river meshes, waterfalls, skybox/cloud systems, toon fog, palette shift, depth noise, bloom, brightness/contrast, and vignette.

### Problem

Reproducing the full R3F postprocessing and river/waterfall rendering stack would materially expand the first conversion pass while adding little to the Bomberman core loop.

### StemStudio Limitation

The port targets a validator-clean StemStudio bundle with runtime-only WebGPU-safe primitives and UIKit. The source React component/postprocessing stack cannot be imported directly.

### Gameplay Impact

Visual atmosphere is simplified. Gameplay-critical movement, bombs, enemies, pickups, and level progression are preserved.

### Options

#### Option A: Best Fidelity
Port the full voxel river pipeline and postprocessing stack. Highest visual similarity, high implementation risk.

#### Option B: Best Stem-Native
Use scene fog, bloom, gradient background, procedural clouds, colored terrain, and arched bridges. Medium visual fidelity with stable runtime behavior.

#### Option C: Fastest Fallback
Flat grid arena with no islands/clouds. Fast but loses the source identity.

### Decision

Option B. Keep the sky-island identity and bridge traversal while deferring exact water/post effects.

### Consequences

- The first pass is playable and easier to validate.
- Rivers/waterfalls remain a follow-up visual fidelity item.
- Camera and world tuning attributes provide some art-direction flexibility.

### Files Affected

- `imports/sky-bomber-runtime.js`
- `sky-bomber.stemscript`

# 100 cars

This bundle was generated by the Script Tool scene exporter.

## Quick Start

1. Open the Script Tool terminal in StemStudio.
2. Run `exec` and choose this bundle folder.
3. Execute `100-cars.stemscript`.

## Bundle Layout

- `100-cars.stemscript`: main Script Tool entrypoint
- `behaviors/`: exported behavior YAML files
- `lambdas/`: exported lambda YAML files and lambda binding data
- `models/`, `textures/`, `audio/`, `videos/`: exported binary assets
- `conversion-plan.md`, `conversion-notes.md`, `source-map.md`, `source-analysis.json`: export metadata

## Important

- Model imports are emitted per scene object for reliable Script Tool replay. Re-importing repeated model files will create repeated assets unless you deduplicate them manually afterward.
- Lambda bindings are exported for reference, but the current Script Tool does not automatically recreate them.
- Direct material texture assignments and some advanced behavior references may still need manual relinking after import.

# Red Runner - StemStudio Example

A 3D side-scrolling infinite runner inspired by [BayatGames/RedRunner](https://github.com/BayatGames/RedRunner). The player auto-runs to the right, jumps over obstacles, collects coins, and survives as long as possible.

All behaviors and lambdas are provided as **importable `.yaml` files** — drag them straight into the StemStudio editor.

## Game Overview

| Mechanic | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Auto-Run | Player moves right automatically at a configurable speed |
| Jump | Press **Space**, **W**, or **Arrow Up** to jump |
| Obstacles | Saws (spinning), spikes (static), maces (swinging) kill on contact |
| Coins | Collect for bonus score |
| Scoring | Distance traveled + coins collected |
| Death | Touching an obstacle or falling off the level |
| Restart | Auto-restarts 2 seconds after death |

---

## Importing Behaviors & Lambdas

1. Open StemStudio
2. Open the **Assets Panel** (left sidebar)
3. Click **Import** next to "Behaviors" and select each `.yaml` file from `behaviors/`:
   - `redRunnerGame.yaml`
   - `redRunnerPlayer.yaml`
   - `redRunnerCamera.yaml`
   - `redRunnerObstacle.yaml`
   - `redRunnerCoin.yaml`
4. Click **Import** next to "Lambdas" and select each `.yaml` file from `lambdas/`:
   - `spin.yaml`
   - `oscillate.yaml`

See [docs/yaml-import-export.md](../../docs/yaml-import-export.md) for the YAML format specification.

---

## Extracted Assets

All original Red Runner assets have been downloaded into `assets/`:

### Sprites (`assets/sprites/` — 3.8 MB, 298 files)

| Folder | Contents | Use For |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `RedRunner/Characters/Red/` | Body, Eye, Arms, Feet, Hands (8 PNGs) | Player character texture reference |
| `RedRunner/Tiles_256/` | Grass, Dirt, Columns, Hills (34 PNGs) | Ground/platform textures |
| `RedRunner/Enemies/` | Saw, Mace, Spike, Eye sprites (19 PNGs) | Obstacle textures |
| `RedRunner/Coin/` | Coin sprite sheet + frames (18 PNGs) | Coin textures |
| `RedRunner/Background/` | Clouds, blocks, sky layers (20 PNGs) | Parallax background textures |
| `RedRunner/Environment/All/` | Trees, bushes, mushrooms in 1x/2x/4x (45 PNGs) | Decoration textures |
| `RedRunner/Water/` | Water animation frames (17 PNGs) | Water hazard texture |

### Audio (`assets/audio/` — 97 MB, 34 files)

| Folder | Contents | Use For |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `Character/Footstep/` | 20 footstep variations (.wav) | Running sound effects |
| `Character/Jump/` | Jump.wav | Jump sound |
| `Collectable/` | Coin.wav, Chest.wav | Collection sound effects |
| `Enemies/` | Saw.wav, Spike.wav, Mace Slam, Water (.wav) | Obstacle sound effects |
| `Environment/` | Birds.ogg, Wind.mp3 | Ambient background audio |

---

## Scene Setup Guide

### Step 1: Create a New Scene

Open StemStudio and create a new, empty scene. Set background to `#87CEEB` (sky blue).

### Step 2: Add Lighting

1. **Directional Light** — Position: `(5, 10, 5)`, Color: `#FFFFFF`, Intensity: `1.0`
2. **Ambient Light** — Color: `#B0C4DE`, Intensity: `0.4`

### Step 3: Create the Game Manager

1. Create an **Empty Object**, name it `GameManager`, position `(0, 0, 0)`
2. **Attach behavior**: `Red Runner - Game Manager`

### Step 4: Create the Player

1. Create a **Box** — Size: `1 x 1.5 x 1`, Position: `(0, 2, 0)`, Color: `#E74C3C` (red)
2. Name it `Player`
3. **Attach behavior**: `Red Runner - Player`
4. Attributes:

| Attribute | Value | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| Run Speed | `8` | Units per second |
| Jump Force | `12` | Jump height |
| Gravity | `30` | Fall speed |
| Ground Check Distance | `1.2` | Slightly more than half player height |
| Death Y Level | `-10` | Fall death threshold |

### Step 5: Create Ground Platforms

Create **Box** objects along the X axis:

| Name | Size | Position | Color |
|------|------|----------|-------|
| Ground_Start | `30 x 1 x 4` | `(10, 0, 0)` | `#8B4513` |
| Ground_Mid1 | `20 x 1 x 4` | `(40, 0, 0)` | `#8B4513` |
| Ground_High1 | `10 x 1 x 4` | `(70, 3, 0)` | `#228B22` |
| Ground_Mid2 | `15 x 1 x 4` | `(90, 0, 0)` | `#8B4513` |

Layout:
```
X:  0----25   30----50   55----65   68----80   85---100
Y:  0         0          3          0          0
    [Start]   [Mid1]     [High]     [Mid2]     [Long]
```

### Step 6: Create Obstacles

#### Saw (Spinning)
- **Cylinder** — Radius: `0.8`, Height: `0.2`, Color: `#808080`, Position: `(20, 1.5, 0)`
- Behavior: `Red Runner - Obstacle` — Kill Radius: `1.0`, Type: Saw
- Lambda: `Spin` — Speed Z: `5`

#### Spike (Static)
- **Cone** — Radius: `0.4`, Height: `1.0`, Color: `#696969`, Position: `(35, 1, 0)`
- Behavior: `Red Runner - Obstacle` — Kill Radius: `0.8`, Type: Spike

#### Mace (Swinging)
- **Sphere** — Radius: `0.6`, Color: `#4A4A4A`, Position: `(60, 4, 0)`
- Behavior: `Red Runner - Obstacle` — Kill Radius: `1.2`, Type: Mace
- Lambda: `Oscillate` — Axis: Y, Amplitude: `2`, Frequency: `0.8`

### Step 7: Create Coins

- **Cylinder** (flat disc) — Radius: `0.3`, Height: `0.05`, Color: `#FFD700`, Position: floating above platforms
- Behavior: `Red Runner - Coin` — Collect Radius: `1.5`, Value: `1`
- Lambda: `Spin` — Speed Y: `3`

Place in lines along platforms:
```
(5, 2, 0)   (7, 2, 0)   (9, 2, 0)   (11, 2, 0)   (13, 2, 0)
```

Or as arcs over gaps:
```
(27, 2.5, 0)   (28.5, 3.5, 0)   (30, 4, 0)   (31.5, 3.5, 0)   (33, 2.5, 0)
```

### Step 8: Set Up the Camera

1. Select the scene **Camera**, position: `(0, 4, 12)`
2. **Attach behavior**: `Red Runner - Camera`

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Offset X | `-2` |
| Offset Y | `4` |
| Offset Z | `12` |
| Smooth Speed | `5` |
| Look At Player | `true` |

### Step 9: Play!

Press **Play**. Score and game state are logged to the browser console.

---

## Full Object Checklist

| Object | Shape | Color | Position | Behaviors | Lambdas |
|--------|-------|-------|----------|-----------|---------|
| GameManager | Empty | - | `(0,0,0)` | `redRunnerGame` | - |
| Player | Box 1x1.5x1 | `#E74C3C` | `(0,2,0)` | `redRunnerPlayer` | - |
| Ground_Start | Box 30x1x4 | `#8B4513` | `(10,0,0)` | - | - |
| Ground_Mid1 | Box 20x1x4 | `#8B4513` | `(40,0,0)` | - | - |
| Ground_High1 | Box 10x1x4 | `#228B22` | `(70,3,0)` | - | - |
| Saw_1 | Cylinder r=0.8 | `#808080` | `(20,1.5,0)` | `redRunnerObstacle` | `spin` (Z=5) |
| Spike_1 | Cone r=0.4 | `#696969` | `(35,1,0)` | `redRunnerObstacle` | - |
| Mace_1 | Sphere r=0.6 | `#4A4A4A` | `(60,4,0)` | `redRunnerObstacle` | `oscillate` (Y, amp=2) |

| Camera | Camera | - | `(0,4,12)` | `redRunnerCamera` | - |

---

## Architecture

### Communication Flow

```
[Player presses Space]
    -> RedRunnerPlayer: jump on next update()

[Player touches obstacle]
    -> RedRunnerObstacle: distance < killRadius
    -> sends "die" to RedRunnerPlayer
    -> sends "shake" to RedRunnerCamera
    -> RedRunnerPlayer sends "playerDied" to RedRunnerGame
    -> RedRunnerGame: state = "gameOver", logs score
    -> After 2s: sends "restart" to all players/coins/obstacles

[Player touches coin]
    -> RedRunnerCoin: distance < collectRadius -> hides coin
    -> sends "coinCollected" to RedRunnerGame
```

### Shared State (`erth.store`)

| Key | Type | Description |
|-----|------|-------------|
| `redRunner.state` | `"idle" \| "playing" \| "gameOver"` | Current game state |
| `redRunner.score` | `number` | Distance-based score |
| `redRunner.coins` | `number` | Coins collected |
| `redRunner.highScore` | `number` | Best score across restarts |
| `redRunner.playerUuid` | `string` | Player object UUID |

---

## File Structure

```
examples/red-runner/
├── README.md
├── behaviors/
│   ├── redRunnerGame.yaml         <- Import into editor
│   ├── redRunnerPlayer.yaml
│   ├── redRunnerCamera.yaml
│   ├── redRunnerObstacle.yaml
│   └── redRunnerCoin.yaml
├── lambdas/
│   ├── spin.yaml                  <- Import into editor
│   └── oscillate.yaml
└── assets/
    ├── sprites/                   <- 298 PNG files from Red Runner
    └── audio/                     <- 34 audio files from Red Runner
```

    - lambda

      name: speedX

      invisible: false
      default: 0
      order: 0
    speedY:
      name: speedY
      type: number
      array: false

    speedZ:
      name: speedZ
      type: number

code: |
  // Spin Lambda
  // Applies continuous rotation to all registered objects.
  // Use for coins (Y-axis) and saws (Z-axis).

  function update(deltaTime) {
      if (!deltaTime) deltaTime = 0.016;

      this.processObjects(deltaTime, function(object, data, dt) {
          object.rotation.x += (data.speedX === undefined ? 0 : data.speedX) * dt;
          object.rotation.y += (data.speedY === undefined ? 2 : data.speedY) * dt;
          object.rotation.z += (data.speedZ === undefined ? 0 : data.speedZ) * dt;
      });
  }
